cargo theft risk prediction production case study false positives logistics
notes: No opportunity published. Cargo-theft risk prediction and trip-exception orchestration had strong current Brazilian problem evidence: public authorities and transport associations report persistent theft, concentrated corridors, high-value targets, and coordinated prevention needs. However, the round found no load-bearing Brazilian production evaluation isolating the incremental contribution of machine learning from GPS tracking, locks, route rules, insurer requirements, police integration, and conventional risk-management controls. The strongest local technology claim attributed a large decline to combined public-private cooperation and technology rather than a measured model effect. Foreign research demonstrated predictive modeling feasibility but not Brazilian production economics, false-alert burden, driver acceptance, or causal loss reduction. Under the configured solution-evidence and feasibility gates, the candidate remained an unmeasured pilot-level hypothesis and was not published. Advanced cursor to agriculture.

precision agriculture AI failed pilot false positives adoption cost Brazil
agricultural yield forecasting AI production deployment Brazil measured results
precision agriculture discontinued failed adoption connectivity cost small farms
notes: No opportunity published. The candidate was a field-level crop-risk forecasting and precision-intervention platform combining weather, remote sensing, machinery, and farm observations. Current Brazilian sources establish agricultural scale and material climate-driven productivity variability, but they do not establish a comparable Brazilian production deployment with measured incremental benefit over agronomic scouting, weather alerts, soil sampling, deterministic thresholds, and existing precision-agriculture tools. Counter-evidence and recent deployment reviews identify connectivity constraints, heterogeneous hardware, weak reproducibility, inconsistent resource profiling, integration cost, and limited suitability for smaller farms. The solution remained a broad platform hypothesis with unclear labels, causal value, unit economics, false-alert burden, and farmer adoption. Under the configured due-diligence gates, it was not published. Advanced cursor to construction-real-estate; next agriculture focus shifts to post-harvest quality, storage losses, input-application evidence, livestock health, and traceability.
